paragraphs.text:
| Highlight | Data (NIST SRD‑46) |
|---|---|
| Lanthanide contraction shows up in citrate binding at matched conditions | At 25 °C, I ≈ 0.1 M (aqueous), the 1:1 citrate complexes strengthen across the series. Typical logβ1 values trend La < Gd < Lu, with Y(III) tracking near the mid-late lanthanides. |
| Oxidation-state quirks | Cerium has both Ce(III) and the uncommon Ce(IV) in the database; Eu(II) appears but with very sparse water/25 °C entries. These unusual states demand careful handling because hydrolysis and redox can dominate. |
| Coverage snapshot | The Ln(III) ions have extensive records: e.g., La(III) metal_79 has ligand_totN_297 and vlm_totN_931; Gd(III) metal_65 has 288/897; Lu(III) metal_81 has 234/671; Y(III) metal_205 has 182/—. Ce(IV) metal_28 has far fewer complexes (ligand_totN_3), reflecting its hydrolytic/redox sensitivity. |

claims.text: | Lanthanide contraction shows up in citrate binding at matched conditions | At 25 °C, I ≈ 0.1 M (aqueous), the 1:1 citrate complexes strengthen across the series. Typical logβ1 values trend La < Gd < Lu, with Y(III) tracking near the mid-late lanthanides. |

| Oxidation-state quirks | Cerium has both Ce(III) and the uncommon Ce(IV) in the database; Eu(II) appears but with very sparse water/25 °C entries. These unusual states demand careful handling because hydrolysis and redox can dominate. |

| Coverage snapshot | The Ln(III) ions have extensive records: e.g., La(III) metal_79 has ligand_totN_297 and vlm_totN_931; Gd(III) metal_65 has 288/897; Lu(III) metal_81 has 234/671; Y(III) metal_205 has 182/—. Ce(IV) metal_28 has far fewer complexes (ligand_totN_3), reflecting its hydrolytic/redox sensitivity. |

paragraphs.text:
Notes and insight
- The citrate series cleanly illustrates the lanthanide contraction: as the 3+ cations get smaller from La to Lu, hard O‑donor ligands bind more strongly. Y(III), being similar in size to Ho–Er, typically sits in the late‑Ln range.
- SRD‑46 shows many matched-condition entries for citrate across Ln(III), making it a reliable comparison set.
- Unusual oxidation states: Eu(II) and Ce(IV) are present but sparse under strictly aqueous, ~25 °C, 1:1 filters. Ce(IV) tends to hydrolyze; Eu(II) is air‑sensitive and often measured under specialized media.

claims.text: Notes and insight
The citrate series cleanly illustrates the lanthanide contraction
as the 3+ cations get smaller from La to Lu
hard O‑donor ligands bind more strongly.
Y(III), being similar in size to Ho–Er,
typically sits in the late‑Ln range.
SRD‑46 shows many matched-condition entries for citrate across Ln(III)
making it a reliable comparison set.
Unusual oxidation states:
Eu(II) and Ce(IV) are present
but sparse under strictly aqueous, ~25 °C, 1:1 filters.
Ce(IV) tends to hydrolyze;
Eu(II) is air‑sensitive
and often measured under specialized media.

grounded_claims.evidence_snippet: Filler span: no useful database-groundable information.
Lanthanide contraction is established chemistry; not explicitly stated in SRD-46 rows.
Decrease in Ln(III) ionic radii from La to Lu is standard knowledge, not a database row.
HSAB principle: hard O‑donors bind more strongly to hard 3+ cations; not a direct SRD-46 datum.
Y(III) ionic radius ~Ho–Er is accepted chemistry; SRD-46 does not list ionic radii.
Y(III) typically behaves like late lanthanides; this is not explicitly stated in the database.
